title: min-btc-node
description: A minimalist, lightweight Bitcoin node implementation built from scratch in Rust. Handles low-level networking and protocol rules. Engineered to deeply understand concurrent peer connections and precise binary protocol serialization.

title: nixos-config
description: My declarative NixOS system configurations and dotfiles, allowing fully reproducible developer environments and system states via Infrastructure as Code. Solves the 'it works on my machine' problem at the OS level.

title: pixel-war
description: A Web3 pixel-canvas decentralized application (dApp). Users can bid on-chain to own and color pixels, claim refunds when outbid, and mint ERC-721 snapshots of the grid. Demonstrated ability to handle complex on-chain state and smart contract security.

title: HomeLab Infrastructure
description: A domestic server that hosts various services such as a JellyFin instance, a Gitea instance, and Pi-hole. The server runs Unraid as the host OS and uses Docker for containerization. Built to learn bare-metal provisioning and network routing.
